mission.title: Install and Configure Telepresence on Kubernetes
mission.description: Telepresence is a tool that allows you to connect your local workstation to a remote Kubernetes or OpenShift cluster, enabling you to run services locally while accessing cluster resources. This setup facilitates fast local development without the need for lengthy container build and deployment cycles.

steps.title: Add the Telepresence Helm repository
steps.description:
Add the official Telepresence Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration. This repository contains the necessary charts for deploying Telepresence components.
```bash
helm repo add telepresence-oss https://charts.telepresence.io
helm repo update
```

steps.title: Install the Telepresence Traffic Manager
steps.description:
Deploy the Telepresence Traffic Manager to your Kubernetes cluster using Helm. Make sure to specify the namespace where you want to install it.
```bash
helm install telepresence-traffic-manager telepresence-oss/telepresence-oss --namespace telepresence --create-namespace --version 1.1.1-bogus.overwritten.by.chart.go
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check that the Telepresence Traffic Manager pod is running successfully. This step ensures that the installation was successful and that the service is operational.
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace telepresence
```

steps.title: Configure resource limits
steps.description:
Set resource limits for the Telepresence Traffic Manager to ensure it runs efficiently within your cluster. Edit the values.yaml file or use `--set` flags to specify resource limits during installation.
```bash
helm upgrade telepresence-traffic-manager telepresence-oss/telepresence-oss --namespace telepresence --set resources.limits.cpu=500m --set resources.limits.memory=256Mi
```

steps.title: Access the Telepresence Traffic Manager
steps.description:
You can port-forward the Traffic Manager service to access it locally. This allows you to interact with the service from your local machine.
```bash
kubectl port-forward svc/telepresence-traffic-manager --namespace telepresence 8081:8081
```

resolution.summary: A successful installation of Telepresence will result in the Traffic Manager pod running in the specified namespace, and you will be able to access it via port forwarding. You can now start developing locally while interacting with your remote Kubernetes cluster.
codeSnippets:
helm repo add telepresence-oss https://charts.telepresence.io
helm repo update
helm upgrade telepresence-traffic-manager telepresence-oss/telepresence-oss --namespace telepresence --set resources.limits.cpu=500m --set resources.limits.memory=256Mi

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running and both Helm and kubectl installed on your local machine.
